preview_text:
The model favors France at 55%, with Norway's win probability at 17%. This represents a substantial gap; France enters as the clear favorite on the neutral ground, though the underdog retains a meaningful chance to pull an upset.

The 28% draw probability sits between the two decisive outcomes, suggesting the match carries competitive texture. France's 37-point lead over Norway in win probability indicates the model expects France to control play, yet the draw sitting ahead of a Norway victory signals neither team is heavily skewed toward blowout outcomes. A close, tightly contested match remains the second-most-likely scenario.

The model and sportsbook consensus are closely aligned across all three outcomes. The market places France slightly lower at 52% (a 3-point gap), Norway marginally higher at 22%, and the draw 1 point lower. Polymarket pricing sits between the two. These small divergences suggest broad agreement on the match structure, with no sharp disagreement worth flagging.
